---

# PESER — Powszechny Elektroniczny System Ewidencji Robotów
## Comprehensive Implementation Plan

---

## 1. Architecture Overview and Design Philosophy

PESER is a multi-page React SPA that mirrors MERA Robotics' design system exactly: React 19 + Vite + React Router 7, Tailwind CSS v4 (inline `@theme` in `index.css`, no config file), Framer Motion 12, lucide-react icons, Space Grotesk font. The aesthetic is institutional gravitas — the same pure-black/white/opacity-only palette, zero border-radius, film grain overlay, and WebGL backgrounds — but now applied to a government-grade registration system rather than a commercial product.

The site has two registers of content: a public marketing/informational layer (pages 1–3, 8–10) and a technical/standards layer (pages 4–7). This distinction drives both the routing strategy and the copy register.

---

## 2. PESER Number Format Design

The PESER number must look credibly official and carry structured machine-readable information. Proposed format:

```
PL-[YEAR][MONTH]-[CLASS]-[SEQ]-[CHK]
Example: PL-202504-H1-00001-X7
```

- `PL` — country code (ISO 3166-1, hardcoded for Poland)
- `202504` — registration year + month (YYYYMM)
- `H1` — risk class code: H1 (humanoid public), H2 (humanoid restricted), I1 (industrial cobot), I2 (industrial full), AV (autonomous vehicle), Q1 (quadruped service)
- `00001` — zero-padded 5-digit sequential registry number within that class/month
- `X7` — 2-character Luhn-style checksum computed over preceding segments

This gives 16 visible characters, groups naturally with hyphens, and is visually distinctive from PESEL (which is 11 digits, no separators). It encodes temporal, geographic, and classification data directly in the identifier itself — which makes it excellent for the "digital passport card" visualization.

---

## 3. File Structure

```
peser/
├── index.html
├── vite.config.js
├── package.json
├── .env
├── public/
│   ├── peser-logo.svg           # PESER wordmark / emblem
│   ├── robot-hero.png           # Hero robot imagery (dark-bg optimized)
│   ├── robot-id-card-bg.png     # Background texture for ID card mockup
│   └── favicon.svg
└── src/
    ├── main.jsx
    ├── App.jsx
    ├── index.css                # @import tailwindcss + @theme + film grain + scrollbar
    ├── i18n.jsx                 # LangContext/Provider/useLang — identical pattern to MERA
    ├── lib/
    │   └── supabase.js          # Nullable Supabase client (same pattern as MERA)
    ├── data/
    │   └── registry.js          # Static JSON mock of 20-30 registered robots
    ├── hooks/
    │   └── useScrollFadeIn.js   # Reusable framer-motion inView hook
    ├── components/
    │   │   # Shared primitives — copied/adapted from MERA
    │   ├── ShinyText.jsx         # Identical to MERA
    │   ├── BlurText.jsx          # Identical to MERA
    │   ├── DecryptedText.jsx     # Extracted from MERA's Hero.jsx inline version
    │   ├── AnimatedNumber.jsx    # Extracted from MERA's Stats.jsx inline version
    │   │   # Layout
    │   ├── Navbar.jsx            # PESER-branded, links to page routes
    │   ├── Footer.jsx            # PESER footer
    │   ├── PageShell.jsx         # Consistent page wrapper with padding/maxWidth
    │   ├── SectionLabel.jsx      # Reusable micro-label (UPPERCASE, 0.15em tracking)
    │   ├── HairlineDivider.jsx   # 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.06) divider
    │   │   # Backgrounds
    │   ├── LiquidEther.jsx       # Copied verbatim from MERA (Three.js fluid sim)
    │   ├── SoftAurora.jsx        # Copied verbatim from MERA (OGL GLSL aurora)
    │   ├── GridBackground.jsx    # New: CSS-only perspective grid for registry/cert pages
    │   │   # UI Atoms
    │   ├── PeserBadge.jsx        # PESER number formatted with monospace + hyphen groups
    │   ├── RiskClassBadge.jsx    # Colored status pill: AKTYWNY/ZAWIESZONY/UNIEWAŻNIONY
    │   ├── StatusIndicator.jsx   # Pulsing dot + text for robot operational status
    │   └── RobotIdCard.jsx       # The "digital passport" card visualization
    └── pages/
        ├── Landing.jsx          # Page 1: Hero + intro stats
        ├── SystemPeser.jsx      # Page 2: What/Why/How in 3 steps
        ├── Rejestr.jsx          # Page 3: Searchable registry mockup
        ├── CyfrowaTozsamosc.jsx # Page 4: DID/SSI/crypto keys
        ├── Certyfikacja.jsx     # Page 5: Zero Trust + Technical Passport
        ├── Klasyfikacja.jsx     # Page 6: Risk profiles
        ├── Transparentnosc.jsx  # Page 7: Blockchain audit log mockup
        ├── PanelRejestracji.jsx # Page 8: Registration form
        ├── DlaInwestorow.jsx    # Page 9: Investment pitch
        └── KontaktApi.jsx       # Page 10: Developer API + government contact
```

---

## 4. Routing Strategy

PESER is a true multi-page application, not a single-scroll site like MERA. React Router 7 with `createBrowserRouter` handles this. The Navbar links to path-based routes rather than hash anchors. A `PageShell` wrapper component provides consistent enter animations.

```
/             → Landing (Hero)
/system       → SystemPeser
/rejestr      → Rejestr (public database)
/rejestr/:id  → Individual robot detail page (RobotIdCard fullscreen)
/tozsamosc    → CyfrowaTozsamosc
/certyfikacja → Certyfikacja
/klasyfikacja → Klasyfikacja
/transparentnosc → Transparentnosc
/rejestracja  → PanelRejestracji
/inwestorzy   → DlaInwestorow
/kontakt      → KontaktApi
```

Language prefixing: `/pl/rejestr`, `/en/rejestr` — same URL-path detection as MERA's `detectLang()`. The `LangProvider` wraps the entire app identically.

---

## 5. Component Architecture — Detailed Specifications

### 5.1 Navbar.jsx
Identical mechanics to MERA's Navbar: fixed, transparent until scroll > 50px, then `rgba(0,0,0,0.85)` + `backdrop-filter: blur(20px)`. Navigation links are now `<Link>` components pointing to routes instead of `href="#anchor"`. The CTA button reads "ZAREJESTRUJ ROBOTA" (primary white solid) instead of "Get in Touch". The language toggle works identically.

### 5.2 LiquidEther.jsx — Hero Background
Copy verbatim from MERA. For PESER's hero, use the PESER accent colors:
```js
colors={['#1e38fa', '#5211e8', '#3a7aff']}
```
This gives the electric blue/deep violet fluid — fitting for an identity system. Same auto-demo props.

### 5.3 SoftAurora.jsx — Section Backgrounds
Copy verbatim from MERA. Use on `CyfrowaTozsamosc` and `Certyfikacja` sections:
```js
color1='#1e38fa' color2='#5211e8' brightness={0.7}
```

### 5.4 GridBackground.jsx (New Component)
A CSS-only perspective grid using SVG or CSS transform tricks — a receding grid plane that suggests a data infrastructure or blockchain ledger. This is lighter than WebGL and appropriate for the registry/transparency pages. Implemented as:
- A div with `perspective: 800px` containing an absolutely-positioned SVG grid pattern
- Grid lines at `rgba(255,255,255,0.04)` opacity
- Slow continuous `translateY` animation via CSS keyframes to create the illusion of data flowing toward the viewer

### 5.5 RobotIdCard.jsx — The Digital Passport Visual
This is the centerpiece "wow" component of PESER. It renders an ID-card-proportioned rectangle (85.6mm × 54mm ratio, approximately 560px × 352px) styled as:

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ PESER    [PL emblem]    [robot silhouette] │
│ ─────────────────────────────────────────  │
│ NUMER PESER                                │
│ PL-202504-H1-00001-X7  (monospace, large)  │
│                                            │
│ ROBOT: [Name]           KLASA RYZYKA: H1   │
│ WŁAŚCICIEL: [Corp Name] STATUS: AKTYWNY    │
│ DATA REJ.: 2025-04-15   WYGASA: 2027-04-14 │
│                                            │
│ [QR Code placeholder]  [DID hash truncated]│
└────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Styling: `background: rgba(255,255,255,0.03)`, `border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12)`, very subtle top-to-bottom gradient overlay to simulate card material. A ShinyText sweep runs across the PESER number. The card face contains a scan-line flicker animation (CSS `@keyframes`) at 5% opacity to suggest a live display terminal. On hover, the card scales to 1.02 and border brightens. A `RobotIdCard` in "compact" mode is used in the Rejestr table rows; "full" mode is used on the individual robot detail page.

### 5.6 PeserBadge.jsx
A pure formatting component. Takes a PESER number string and renders it:
- Font: monospace fallback (Space Grotesk has no mono variant; use `font-family: 'Space Mono', monospace` as secondary)
- Groups: each segment separated by a subtle `rgba(255,255,255,0.15)` hyphen
- Optionally shows a copy-to-clipboard icon (lucide `Copy` icon) on hover

### 5.7 RiskClassBadge.jsx
```
H1 → border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.3), text: white  (Humanoid Public)
H2 → border: 1px solid rgba(255,165,0,0.4),   text: rgba(255,165,0,0.8) (Humanoid Restricted)
I1 → border: 1px solid rgba(100,200,255,0.3),  text: rgba(100,200,255,0.8) (Cobot)
I2 → border: 1px solid rgba(255,80,80,0.3),    text: rgba(255,80,80,0.8) (Industrial Full)
AV → border: 1px solid rgba(180,100,255,0.3),  text: rgba(180,100,255,0.8) (Autonomous Vehicle)
```
Per the design language: no solid color fills in UI chrome. Borders and text opacity only.

---

## 6. Page Specifications

### Page 1: Landing.jsx
Structure mirrors MERA's `Landing` function exactly:
- Full-viewport hero section with `LiquidEther` background using blue/violet colors
- Left column: BlurText micro-label "SYSTEM REJESTRACJI ROBOTÓW • POLSKA", then H1 "KAŻDY ROBOT. JEDEN SYSTEM." with `ShinyText` on line 1-2, `DecryptedText` on line 3 ("BEZPIECZEŃSTWO DLA WSZYSTKICH."), then body copy, then two CTA buttons ("PRZEGLĄDAJ REJESTR" white solid + "ZAREJESTRUJ ROBOTA" ghost border)
- Right column: robot imagery with fade gradients identical to MERA's Edward image treatment
- Below the fold: 4-stat bar (`AnimatedNumber`): "1 247 Zarejestrowanych Robotów | 48 Klas Bezpieczeństwa | 12 Województw | 3 Lata Systemu"
- Scroll indicator with `ChevronDown` bounce animation

### Page 2: SystemPeser.jsx
- Section label: "CZYM JEST PESER"
- H2 with split-color pattern: "System, który" + dim "definiuje przyszłość"
- Two-column description: left = 4 short paragraphs about PESER's mission; right = 3-step process cards
- 3-step cards in vertical stack: "1. ZŁÓŻ WNIOSEK → 2. WERYFIKACJA HARDWARE → 3. OTRZYMAJ NUMER PESER", each card with `background: rgba(255,255,255,0.02)`, `border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06)`, lucide icon, title, body
- Below: a full-width `RobotIdCard` in "showcase" orientation (slightly tilted, large, centered) with shimmer animation — the first "wow" moment of the site

### Page 3: Rejestr.jsx
This is the most complex page. Architecture:

**State:** `searchQuery`, `filterClass`, `filterStatus`, `sortBy`

**Mock data source:** `src/data/registry.js` — a JS array of 25 robot objects:
```js
{
  id: 'PL-202501-H1-00001-A3',
  name: 'EDWARD WARCHOCKI',
  manufacturer: 'MERA Robotics',
  owner: 'MERA Robotics sp. z o.o.',
  ownerCity: 'Warszawa',
  riskClass: 'H1',
  status: 'AKTYWNY',
  registrationDate: '2025-01-15',
  expiryDate: '2027-01-14',
  didHash: 'did:peser:zQ3sh...',
  certLevel: 'A',
  operationalScope: 'PUBLIC_EVENTS',
}
```
Include a variety of classes (H1, H2, I1, I2, AV), statuses (AKTYWNY, ZAWIESZONY), manufacturers, and cities. This data is purely static JSON — no Supabase required for the public demo. Supabase integration is optional (future phase) and the `lib/supabase.js` client remains nullable.

**Layout:**
- Search input bar (full width, borderless, bottom-border-only style from MERA Contact form)
- Filter pills row: class filter (ALL, H1, H2, I1, I2, AV) + status filter (WSZYSTKIE, AKTYWNY, ZAWIESZONY)
- Result count: `247 robotów zarejestrowanych · 12 wyświetlanych` (dimmed, small)
- Results as a table-like grid: each row is a motion.div card showing PESER number, robot name, owner, city, risk class badge, status indicator, registration date, and a "→" arrow that routes to `/rejestr/:id`
- On mobile: cards stack, fewer columns shown
- `<Link to={`/rejestr/${robot.id}`}>` for drill-down

**Individual robot detail (`/rejestr/:id`):**
- Full `RobotIdCard` at large scale (centered, 80% viewport width max)
- Below: timeline of audit events (mocked), DID hash display, owner details, certifications list

### Page 4: CyfrowaTozsamosc.jsx
- `SoftAurora` background at low brightness
- Explain DID (Decentralized Identifier) + SSI (Self-Sovereign Identity) in plain language
- Three-panel layout: "Klucz Publiczny" | "Klucz Prywatny" | "Certyfikat Mera OS"
- Each panel shows a mock hex key string in monospace, with a copy button
- "Binding do Mera OS" section: a horizontal flow diagram (CSS flexbox arrows) showing Robot Hardware → Mera OS → PESER Registry → Government Database
- Motion animations: each panel fades in with `useInView` + `initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 40 }}`

### Page 5: Certyfikacja.jsx
- Section on Zero Trust architecture: 4-card grid mirroring MERA's `Solutions.jsx` HorizonCard grid
- Certyfikat Techniczny (Technical Passport) section: render a mock "passport document" — styled like a printed certificate, white text on rgba(255,255,255,0.03), with PESER seal, robot name, hardware specs, test dates
- Security levels A/B/C/D as a horizontal stepper/scale visualization
- Icons from lucide: `Shield`, `ShieldCheck`, `Lock`, `Key`, `AlertTriangle`

### Page 6: Klasyfikacja.jsx
- 5 risk profiles in a 2+3 or 3+2 grid layout (each card taller than MERA's Solutions cards)
- Each card: risk class badge top-left, large class code (H1/H2/I1/I2/AV) in 64px monospace dimmed, title, description, example use cases, required certifications
- Interactive: hover reveals "Zobacz wymagania →" text with ArrowRight icon
- Color cues via border only (per design language — no fills)

### Page 7: Transparentnosc.jsx
- `GridBackground` perspective grid as background
- Blockchain audit log mockup: a scrollable terminal-style list of timestamped events
  ```
  2025-04-15 09:14:32  REJ  PL-202504-H1-00001-X7  Rejestracja zaakceptowana
  2025-04-20 14:22:01  UPD  PL-202504-H1-00001-X7  Certyfikat odnowiony
  2025-05-03 08:00:00  INS  PL-202504-H1-00001-X7  Inspekcja — wynik: POZYTYWNY
  ```
  Styled with monospace font, scanline effect, entries staggered in via Framer Motion
- KYC/KYR (Know Your Robot) procedures: 3-step cards
- "Blockchain hash" display: animated hex string

### Page 8: PanelRejestracji.jsx
A multi-step registration form — the most functionally rich page.

**Step 1 — Dane Właściciela:** NIP/KRS number, company name, authorized person, email, phone
**Step 2 — Dane Robota:** manufacturer, model, serial number, production date, hardware specs, classification selection (radio buttons for risk class)
**Step 3 — Dokumenty:** upload fields for certifications (CE marking, manufacturer safety cert) — these are `<input type="file">` elements styled to match the design
**Step 4 — Potwierdzenie:** summary review + submit

UI pattern identical to MERA's Contact form: transparent fields, bottom-border-only, white placeholder text at 0.25 opacity. Multi-step progress indicator at top: 4 numbered steps, active step in white, completed in dimmed white, future in rgba(255,255,255,0.2). On submit, data goes to Supabase `registrations` table (or logs to console if Supabase is not configured).

### Page 9: DlaInwestorow.jsx
Mirrors MERA's `Solutions.jsx` + `GrowthChart.jsx` structure:
- Market size stats: `AnimatedNumber` for "€2.4B rynek" / "340M robotów do 2035" / "First-in-Poland"
- Investment thesis: 3-column layout of key propositions (government mandate, data moat, API licensing fees, Robot-as-a-Service certification fees)
- Robot economy S-curve (reuse `GrowthChart` SVG pattern but PESER-branded, Y-axis = "Zarejestrowane Roboty", "YOU ARE HERE" marker at 2025)
- Deck download CTA (ghost button, `Download` lucide icon)
- Contact investor relations form (simplified 3-field version)

### Page 10: KontaktApi.jsx
Two-column layout (mirrors MERA Contact):
- Left: API documentation preview — mock OpenAPI endpoint cards:
  ```
  GET  /v1/robots/{peser_id}     Pobierz dane robota
  POST /v1/robots/register       Zarejestruj robota
  GET  /v1/robots/search         Przeszukaj rejestr
  POST /v1/verify/certificate    Zweryfikuj certyfikat
  ```
  Each endpoint card styled as a code block: method badge (GET=dim blue, POST=dim green borders only), path in monospace, description
- Right: Standard contact form identical to MERA's (`supabase.from('contacts').insert(...)`)
- Government integration section: logos/placeholder for GovTech Polska, KPRM, Ministerstwo Cyfryzacji

---

## 7. i18n.jsx — Translation Architecture

Extend MERA's `i18n.jsx` pattern exactly. The file grows to ~600 lines with two language objects (`pl`, `en`). Polish is primary and will be complete; English is secondary and serves investor/international outreach. Key additions over MERA:

- All section labels, page titles, CTA text
- Status labels: AKTYWNY/ACTIVE, ZAWIESZONY/SUSPENDED, UNIEWAŻNIONY/REVOKED
- Risk class names and descriptions in both languages
- Form field labels and validation messages
- API endpoint descriptions
- Blockchain/DID technical terminology

---

## 8. Data Architecture — registry.js

The mock registry is a static JavaScript module (`src/data/registry.js`) exporting an array. This avoids any network dependency for the public demo. The array contains 25-30 hand-crafted entries representing diverse robot types, owners, cities, and statuses. Example entries should include MERA Robotics' own robots (Edward Warchocki, Unitree G1, B2 Series) to create a credible showcase. The search/filter logic in `Rejestr.jsx` operates entirely on this array via `.filter()` and `.sort()`.

Future Supabase integration: a `robots` table with identical schema. The component checks `if (supabase)` identically to MERA's Contact form pattern — falls back to static data gracefully.

---

## 9. Animated Backgrounds — Decision Matrix

| Page | Background | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Landing / Hero | `LiquidEther` (Three.js fluid) blue/violet | Maximum impact, same as MERA hero |
| SystemPeser | None (pure black) | Let the 3-step content breathe |
| Rejestr | `GridBackground` (CSS perspective grid) | Data infrastructure metaphor |
| CyfrowaTozsamosc | `SoftAurora` (OGL GLSL) at low brightness | Cryptographic/ethereal feel |
| Certyfikacja | `SoftAurora` at even lower brightness | Technical depth |
| Klasyfikacja | None | Card-heavy, needs clean backdrop |
| Transparentnosc | `GridBackground` with scanline CSS | Blockchain/ledger aesthetic |
| PanelRejestracji | None | Form needs maximum contrast/readability |
| DlaInwestorow | `LiquidEther` for hero section only | Mirrors MERA investment section |
| KontaktApi | None | Clean, functional |

2. Add a `.scanline` utility class for the terminal/blockchain pages:
```css
@keyframes scanline {
  0% { transform: translateY(-100%); }
  100% { transform: translateY(100%); }
}
.scanline::after {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  width: 100%;
  height: 2px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.03);
  animation: scanline 4s linear infinite;
}
```

## 14. Key Design Decisions Resolved

**Q: How to mock the registry?**
Static `registry.js` array for v1 — zero infrastructure, instantly deployable, perfect for demo/investor presentations. The Supabase schema can be designed in parallel (robots table, owners table, certifications table, audit_log table) and wired in v2 when actual registrations are needed. The component already uses the nullable Supabase client pattern from MERA.

**Q: WebGL or GLSL shaders?**
Both already exist in MERA and are copied verbatim. `LiquidEther` (Three.js) for the hero, `SoftAurora` (OGL/GLSL) for the identity/cert pages. The new `GridBackground` is pure CSS — no third WebGL context needed, which keeps performance manageable on pages that already have much content.

**Q: Multi-language structure?**
Identical to MERA's `LangContext` pattern — single `i18n.jsx` file with two flat translation objects. URL path detection (`/pl/` prefix) + browser language fallback. Polish as default (no prefix). English via `/en/` prefix. The `useLang()` hook is used in every component exactly as in MERA.

**Q: How to visualize the robot digital passport?**
The `RobotIdCard` component is a styled HTML/CSS card (not an image) — this means it is text-selectable, copyable, and screen-reader accessible. It renders the PESER number in large monospace type, uses CSS card-like styling (not realistic plastic but the same glass-dark aesthetic), and has a `ShinyText` sweep over the PESER number as the signature animation moment.

**Q: Investment-grade positioning?**
`DlaInwestorow.jsx` uses the same S-curve SVG from MERA's `GrowthChart` but recontextualized for robot population growth. The `AnimatedNumber` counters show market size. The deck download CTA (ghost button) signals there is a full pitch deck behind the site.

[build]
  command = "npm run build"
  publish = "dist"

[[redirects]]
  from = "/*"
  to = "/index.html"
  status = 200
```

This is identical to MERA's Netlify setup (which already has a `netlify.toml`).

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-03-27 21:20:14Z)**_

Design a comprehensive implementation plan for PESER — "Powszechny Elektroniczny System Ewidencji Robotów" (Poland's Universal Electronic Robot Registration System). This is a brand new greenfield project, separate from the MERA Robotics website.

## Context

PESER is Poland's first national humanoid robot registration system, analogous to the human PESEL system. Imagine you are a humanoid robot living among humans: you need a legal identity, a registered owner, safety certifications, operational permissions, and a verifiable record of your actions. PESER is that system.

The user wants this to be a first-in-Poland, potentially national-standard system targeting investors, government, and robotics companies.

## Design Language (from MERA Robotics - must match exactly)

- **Framework:** React 19 + Vite + React Router 7
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS v4 (no config file — inline @theme in index.css)
- **Animation:** Framer Motion 12
- **Icons:** lucide-react
- **Font:** Space Grotesk (Google Fonts), weights 300/400/500/600/700
- **Color palette:** Pure black (#000) background, white (#fff) text, opacity hierarchy only
  - Card bg: rgba(255,255,255,0.02), border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06)
  - No color accents in UI chrome — only in WebGL/shader backgrounds
  - Accent colors for backgrounds: electric blue (#1e38fa), deep violet (#5211e8)
- **Typography:** Negative letter-spacing (-0.03 to -0.04em) on headings, UPPERCASE micro-labels with 0.15-0.2em spacing
- **Corners:** Zero border-radius everywhere (square corners)
- **Buttons:** Solid white with black text (primary), ghost border (secondary)
- **Film grain overlay:** body::after SVG fractalNoise at opacity 0.6
- **Animations:** ShinyText, BlurText, DecryptedText, scroll fade-in via framer-motion
- **Sections:** 160px vertical padding, 1400px max-width, hairline dividers

## System Requirements (robot perspective)

Design a multi-page web application with these sections/pages:

1. **Landing / Hero** — "Każdy Robot. Jeden System. Bezpieczeństwo dla Wszystkich." with dramatic robot imagery blend
2. **System PESER** — What is PESER, why it matters, how it works (3 steps)
3. **Rejestr** (Registry) — Searchable public database mockup showing registered robots with their PESER numbers, owner, status, risk class
4. **Cyfrowa Tożsamość** — DID/SSI explanation, cryptographic key system, Mera OS binding
5. **Certyfikacja** — Zero Trust architecture, Technical Passport, security levels
6. **Klasyfikacja** — Risk profiles (humanoid public, industrial, cobot, autonomous vehicle)
7. **Transparentność** — Blockchain audit log mockup, KYC/KYR procedures
8. **Panel Rejestracji** — Robot registration form (owner verification, hardware binding, classification)
9. **Dla Inwestorów** — Investment pitch, market size, Robot-as-a-Service economy
10. **Kontakt / API** — Developer API access, government integration contact

## Technical Architecture

Recommend the complete file structure, routing strategy, component architecture, and any backend/data requirements.

## Key Design Decisions to Address

1. How to structure the PESER number format (similar to PESEL but for robots)
2. How to visualize the robot identity card / digital passport
3. How to mock the registry database (JSON data? Supabase?)
4. What animated backgrounds to use (WebGL like MERA, or GLSL shaders)
5. How to structure the multi-language support (Polish primary, English secondary)

Please provide a detailed, executable implementation plan with file structure, component list, and build order.

     1→import { useState, useEffect } from 'react'
     2→import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'
     3→import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion'
     4→import { Menu, X } from 'lucide-react'
     5→import { useLang } from '../i18n'
     6→
     7→export default function Navbar() {
     8→  const { lang, setLang, t } = useLang()
     9→  const navigate = useNavigate()
    10→  const [scrolled, setScrolled] = useState(false)
    11→  const [mobileOpen, setMobileOpen] = useState(false)
    12→
    13→  const toggleLang = () => {
    14→    const newLang = lang === 'en' ? 'pl' : 'en'
    15→    setLang(newLang)
    16→    navigate(newLang === 'en' ? '/' : '/pl', { replace: true })
    17→  }
    18→
    19→  const navLinks = [
    20→    { label: t('nav_showcase'), href: '#showcase' },
    21→    { label: t('nav_solutions'), href: '#solutions' },
    22→    { label: t('nav_about'), href: '#about' },
    23→    { label: t('nav_team'), href: '#team' },
    24→    { label: t('nav_contact'), href: '#contact' },
    25→  ]
    26→
    27→  useEffect(() => {
    28→    const onScroll = () => setScrolled(window.scrollY > 50)
    29→    window.addEventListener('scroll', onScroll)
    30→    return () => window.removeEventListener('scroll', onScroll)
    31→  }, [])
    32→
    33→  return (
    34→    <nav
    35→      style={{
    36→        position: 'fixed',
    37→        top: 0,
    38→        left: 0,
    39→        right: 0,
    40→        zIndex: 1000,
    41→        padding: '0 48px',
    42→        height: 72,
    43→        display: 'flex',
    44→        alignItems: 'center',
    45→        justifyContent: 'space-between',
    46→        background: scrolled ? 'rgba(0,0,0,0.85)' : 'transparent',
    47→        backdropFilter: scrolled ? 'blur(20px)' : 'none',
    48→        borderBottom: scrolled ? '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06)' : '1px solid transparent',
    49→        transition: 'all 0.4s ease',
    50→      }}
    51→    >
    52→      <a href="#" style={{ display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', textDecoration: 'none' }}>
    53→        <img
    54→          src="/meralog.svg"
    55→          alt="MERA Robotics"
    56→          style={{ height: 48, width: 'auto' }}
    57→        />
    58→      </a>
    59→
    60→      <div style={{ display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', gap: 40 }}>
    61→        {navLinks.map((link) => (
    62→          <a
    63→            key={link.href}
    64→            href={link.href}
    65→            style={{
    66→              color: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.6)',
    67→              textDecoration: 'none',
    68→              fontSize: 13,
    69→              fontWeight: 500,
    70→              letterSpacing: '0.05em',
    71→              textTransform: 'uppercase',
    72→              transition: 'color 0.2s',
    73→            }}
    74→            onMouseEnter={(e) => e.target.style.color = '#fff'}
    75→            onMouseLeave={(e) => e.target.style.color = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.6)'}
    76→            className="hidden md:block"
    77→          >
    78→            {link.label}
    79→          </a>
    80→        ))}
    81→
    82→        {/* Language toggle */}
    83→        <button
    84→          onClick={toggleLang}
    85→          style={{
    86→            background: 'none',
    87→            border: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2)',
    88→            color: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.6)',
    89→            cursor: 'pointer',
    90→            padding: '6px 14px',
    91→            fontSize: 12,
    92→            fontWeight: 600,
    93→            fontFamily: "'Space Grotesk', sans-serif",
    94→            letterSpacing: '0.08em',
    95→            transition: 'all 0.2s',
    96→          }}
    97→          onMouseEnter={(e) => {
    98→            e.currentTarget.style.borderColor = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.5)'
    99→            e.currentTarget.style.color = '#fff'
   100→          }}
   101→          onMouseLeave={(e) => {
   102→            e.currentTarget.style.borderColor = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.2)'
   103→            e.currentTarget.style.color = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.6)'
   104→          }}
   105→        >
   106→          {lang === 'en' ? 'PL' : 'EN'}
   107→        </button>
   108→
   109→        <a
   110→          href="#contact"
   111→          style={{
   112→            padding: '10px 24px',
   113→            background: '#fff',
   114→            color: '#000',
   115→            fontSize: 13,
   116→            fontWeight: 600,
   117→            letterSpacing: '0.05em',
   118→            textTransform: 'uppercase',
   119→            textDecoration: 'none',
   120→            transition: 'opacity 0.2s',
   121→          }}
   122→          onMouseEnter={(e) => e.target.style.opacity = '0.8'}
   123→          onMouseLeave={(e) => e.target.style.opacity = '1'}
   124→          className="hidden md:block"
   125→        >
   126→          {t('nav_cta')}
   127→        </a>
   128→
   129→        <button
   130→          onClick={() => setMobileOpen(!mobileOpen)}
   131→          style={{
   132→            background: 'none',
   133→            border: 'none',
   134→            color: '#fff',
   135→            cursor: 'pointer',
   136→            padding: 8,
   137→          }}
   138→          className="md:hidden"
   139→        >
   140→          {mobileOpen ? <X size={24} /> : <Menu size={24} />}
   141→        </button>
   142→      </div>
   143→
   144→      <AnimatePresence>
   145→        {mobileOpen && (
   146→          <motion.div
   147→            initial={{ opacity: 0, y: -20 }}
   148→            animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
   149→            exit={{ opacity: 0, y: -20 }}
   150→            style={{

   160→              gap: 24,

   162→            }}
   163→          >
   164→            {navLinks.map((link) => (
   165→              <a
   166→                key={link.href}
   167→                href={link.href}
   168→                onClick={() => setMobileOpen(false)}
   169→                style={{ color: '#fff', textDecoration: 'none', fontSize: 18, fontWeight: 500 }}
   170→              >
   171→                {link.label}

     8→function DecryptedText({ text, speed = 30 }) {
     9→  const [displayed, setDisplayed] = useState('')
    10→  const chars = 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789'
    11→  const idx = useRef(0)

    13→  useEffect(() => {

    15→    setDisplayed('')

    17→      if (idx.current >= text.length) {
    18→        clearInterval(interval)
    19→        return
    20→      }
    21→      idx.current++
    22→      const revealed = text.slice(0, idx.current)
    23→      const scrambled = Array.from({ length: Math.min(3, text.length - idx.current) }, () =>
    24→        chars[Math.floor(Math.random() * chars.length)]
    25→      ).join('')
    26→      setDisplayed(revealed + scrambled)
    27→    }, speed)
    28→    return () => clearInterval(interval)
    29→  }, [text, speed])
    30→
    31→  return <span>{displayed}</span>
    32→}

```text
     1→import { useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→export default function BlurText({
     4→  text = '',
     5→  delay = 50,
     6→  className = '',
     7→  style = {},
     8→}) {
     9→  const [visibleCount, setVisibleCount] = useState(0)
    10→  const words = (text || '').split(' ')
    11→
    12→  useEffect(() => {
    13→    setVisibleCount(0)
    14→    let i = 0
    15→    const interval = setInterval(() => {
    16→      i++
    17→      setVisibleCount(i)
    18→      if (i >= words.length) clearInterval(interval)
    19→    }, delay)
    20→    return () => clearInterval(interval)
    21→  }, [text, delay])
    22→
    23→  return (
    24→    <span className={className} style={{ ...style, display: 'inline' }}>
    25→      {words.map((word, i) => (
    26→        <span
    27→          key={`${text}-${i}`}
    28→          style={{
    29→            display: 'inline-block',
    30→            filter: i < visibleCount ? 'blur(0px)' : 'blur(10px)',
    31→            opacity: i < visibleCount ? 1 : 0,
    32→            transform: i < visibleCount ? 'translateY(0)' : 'translateY(4px)',
    33→            transition: 'filter 0.5s ease, opacity 0.5s ease, transform 0.5s ease',
    34→            marginRight: '0.25em',
    35→          }}
    36→        >
    37→          {word}
    38→        </span>
    39→      ))}
    40→    </span>
    41→  )
    42→}
    43→
```
</details></tool-use>

   110→    function setMouseCoords(x, y, container) {

   112→      const rect = container.getBoundingClientRect();
   113→      if (rect.width === 0 || rect.height === 0) return;
   114→      const nx = (x - rect.left) / rect.width;
   115→      const ny = (y - rect.top) / rect.height;
   116→      mouseCoords.set(nx * 2 - 1, -(ny * 2 - 1));
   117→      mouseMoved = true;
   118→      mouseTimer = setTimeout(() => { mouseMoved = false; }, 100);
   119→    }
   120→
   121→    function isPointInside(cx, cy, container) {
   122→      const rect = container.getBoundingClientRect();
   123→      return cx >= rect.left && cx <= rect.right && cy >= rect.top && cy <= rect.bottom;
   124→    }
   125→
   126→    let lastUserInteraction = performance.now();
   127→
   128→    // Auto driver
   129→    let autoActive = false;
   130→    let autoCurrent = new THREE.Vector2(0, 0);
   131→    let autoTarget = new THREE.Vector2();
   132→    let autoLastTime = performance.now();
   133→    let autoActivationTime = 0;
   134→    const autoMargin = 0.2;
   135→    const autoRampMs = autoRampDuration * 1000;
   136→
   137→    function pickAutoTarget() {
   138→      autoTarget.set((Math.random() * 2 - 1) * (1 - autoMargin), (Math.random() * 2 - 1) * (1 - autoMargin));
   139→    }
   140→    pickAutoTarget();
   141→
   142→    function updateAutoDriver() {
   143→      if (!autoDemo) return;
   144→      const now = performance.now();
   145→      if (now - lastUserInteraction < autoResumeDelay) {
   146→        if (autoActive) { autoActive = false; isAutoActive = false; }
   147→        return;
   148→      }
   149→      if (isHoverInside) {
   150→        if (autoActive) { autoActive = false; isAutoActive = false; }
   151→        return;
   152→      }
   153→      if (!autoActive) {
   154→        autoActive = true;
   155→        autoCurrent.copy(mouseCoords);
   156→        autoLastTime = now;
   157→        autoActivationTime = now;
   158→      }
   159→      isAutoActive = true;
   160→      let dtSec = (now - autoLastTime) / 1000;
   161→      autoLastTime = now;
   162→      if (dtSec > 0.2) dtSec = 0.016;
   163→      const dir = new THREE.Vector2().subVectors(autoTarget, autoCurrent);
   164→      const dist = dir.length();
   165→      if (dist < 0.01) { pickAutoTarget(); return; }
   166→      dir.normalize();
   167→      let ramp = 1;
   168→      if (autoRampMs > 0) {
   169→        const t = Math.min(1, (now - autoActivationTime) / autoRampMs);
   170→        ramp = t * t * (3 - 2 * t);
   171→      }
   172→      const move = Math.min(autoSpeed * dtSec * ramp, dist);
   173→      autoCurrent.addScaledVector(dir, move);
   174→      mouseCoords.set(autoCurrent.x, autoCurrent.y);
   175→      mouseMoved = true;
   176→    }
   177→
   178→    function updateMouse() {
   179→      if (takeoverActiveVal) {
   180→        const t = (performance.now() - takeoverStartTimeVal) / (takeoverDuration * 1000);
   181→        if (t >= 1) {
   182→          takeoverActiveVal = false;
   183→          mouseCoords.copy(takeoverToVal);
   184→          mouseCoords_old.copy(mouseCoords);
   185→          mouseDiff.set(0, 0);
   186→        } else {
   187→          const k = t * t * (3 - 2 * t);
   188→          mouseCoords.copy(takeoverFromVal).lerp(takeoverToVal, k);
   189→        }
   190→      }
   191→      mouseDiff.subVectors(mouseCoords, mouseCoords_old);
   192→      mouseCoords_old.copy(mouseCoords);
   193→      if (mouseCoords_old.x === 0 && mouseCoords_old.y === 0) mouseDiff.set(0, 0);
   194→      if (isAutoActive && !takeoverActiveVal) mouseDiff.multiplyScalar(autoIntensityVal);
   195→    }
   196→
   197→    // Shader pass helper
   198→    function createShaderPass(opts) {
   199→      const scene = new THREE.Scene();
   200→      const camera = new THREE.Camera();
   201→      if (opts.material) {
   202→        const mat = new THREE.RawShaderMaterial(opts.material);
   203→        const geo = new THREE.PlaneGeometry(2, 2);
   204→        scene.add(new THREE.Mesh(geo, mat));
   205→        return { scene, camera, material: mat, uniforms: mat.uniforms, output: opts.output };
   206→      }
   207→      return { scene, camera, output: opts.output };
   208→    }
   209→
   210→    function renderPass(pass) {
   211→      Common_renderer.setRenderTarget(pass.output || null);
   212→      Common_renderer.render(pass.scene, pass.camera);
   213→      Common_renderer.setRenderTarget(null);
   214→    }
   215→
   216→    // Init
   217→    const container = mountRef.current;
   218→    container.style.position = container.style.position || 'relative';
   219→    container.style.overflow = 'hidden';
   220→    commonInit(container);
   221→    container.prepend(Common_renderer.domElement);
   222→
   223→    // FBO setup
   224→    const res = resolution;
   225→    const fboW = Math.max(1, Math.round(res * Common_width));
   226→    const fboH = Math.max(1, Math.round(res * Common_height));
   227→    const cellScale = new THREE.Vector2(1 / fboW, 1 / fboH);
   228→    const fboSize = new THREE.Vector2(fboW, fboH);
   229→    const boundarySpace = new THREE.Vector2();
   230→
   231→    function getFloatType() {
   232→      return /(iPad|iPhone|iPod)/i.test(navigator.userAgent) ? THREE.HalfFloatType : THREE.FloatType;
   233→    }
   234→
   235→    function makeFBO() {
   236→      return new THREE.WebGLRenderTarget(fboW, fboH, {
   237→        type: getFloatType(), depthBuffer: false, stencilBuffer: false,
   238→        minFilter: THREE.LinearFilter, magFilter: THREE.LinearFilter,
   239→        wrapS: THREE.ClampToEdgeWrapping, wrapT: THREE.ClampToEdgeWrapping
   240→      });
   241→    }
   242→
   243→    const fbos = {
   244→      vel_0: makeFBO(), vel_1: makeFBO(),
   245→      vel_viscous0: makeFBO(), vel_viscous1: makeFBO(),
   246→      div: makeFBO(),
   247→      pressure_0: makeFBO(), pressure_1: makeFBO()
   248→    };
   249→
   250→    // Advection
   251→    const advectionPass = createShaderPass({
   252→      material: {
   253→        vertexShader: face_vert, fragmentShader: advection_frag,
   254→        uniforms: {
   255→          boundarySpace: { value: cellScale }, px: { value: cellScale }, fboSize: { value: fboSize },
   256→          velocity: { value: fbos.vel_0.texture }, dt: { value: dt }, isBFECC: { value: true }
   257→        }
   258→      },
   259→      output: fbos.vel_1
   260→    });
   261→
   262→    // Boundary lines for advection
   263→    const bGeo = new THREE.BufferGeometry();
   264→    bGeo.setAttribute('position', new THREE.BufferAttribute(new Float32Array([-1,-1,0,-1,1,0,-1,1,0,1,1,0,1,1,0,1,-1,0,1,-1,0,-1,-1,0]), 3));
   265→    const bMat = new THREE.RawShaderMaterial({ vertexShader: line_vert, fragmentShader: advection_frag, uniforms: advectionPass.uniforms });
   266→    const bLine = new THREE.LineSegments(bGeo, bMat);
   267→    bLine.visible = isBounce;
   268→    advectionPass.scene.add(bLine);
   269→
   270→    // External force (mouse)
   271→    const forceScene = new THREE.Scene();
   272→    const forceCamera = new THREE.Camera();
   273→    const mouseGeo = new THREE.PlaneGeometry(1, 1);
   274→    const mouseMat = new THREE.RawShaderMaterial({
   275→      vertexShader: mouse_vert, fragmentShader: externalForce_frag,
   276→      blending: THREE.AdditiveBlending, depthWrite: false,
   277→      uniforms: {
   278→        px: { value: cellScale },
   279→        force: { value: new THREE.Vector2(0, 0) },
   280→        center: { value: new THREE.Vector2(0, 0) },
   281→        scale: { value: new THREE.Vector2(cursorSize, cursorSize) }
   282→      }
   283→    });
   284→    forceScene.add(new THREE.Mesh(mouseGeo, mouseMat));
   285→
   286→    // Viscous
   287→    const viscousPass = createShaderPass({
   288→      material: {
   289→        vertexShader: face_vert, fragmentShader: viscous_frag,
   290→        uniforms: {
   291→          boundarySpace: { value: boundarySpace },
   292→          velocity: { value: fbos.vel_1.texture },
   293→          velocity_new: { value: fbos.vel_viscous0.texture },
   294→          v: { value: viscous }, px: { value: cellScale }, dt: { value: dt }
   295→        }
   296→      },
   297→      output: fbos.vel_viscous1
   298→    });
   299→
   300→    // Divergence
   301→    const divPass = createShaderPass({
   302→      material: {
   303→        vertexShader: face_vert, fragmentShader: divergence_frag,
   304→        uniforms: {
   305→          boundarySpace: { value: boundarySpace },
   306→          velocity: { value: fbos.vel_viscous0.texture },
   307→          px: { value: cellScale }, dt: { value: dt }
   308→        }
   309→      },
   310→      output: fbos.div
   311→    });
   312→
   313→    // Poisson
   314→    const poissonPass = createShaderPass({
   315→      material: {
   316→        vertexShader: face_vert, fragmentShader: poisson_frag,
   317→        uniforms: {
   318→          boundarySpace: { value: boundarySpace },
   319→          pressure: { value: fbos.pressure_0.texture },
   320→          divergence: { value: fbos.div.texture },
   321→          px: { value: cellScale }
   322→        }
   323→      },
   324→      output: fbos.pressure_1
   325→    });
   326→
   327→    // Pressure
   328→    const pressurePass = createShaderPass({
   329→      material: {
   330→        vertexShader: face_vert, fragmentShader: pressure_frag,
   331→        uniforms: {
   332→          boundarySpace: { value: boundarySpace },
   333→          pressure: { value: fbos.pressure_0.texture },
   334→          velocity: { value: fbos.vel_viscous0.texture },
   335→          px: { value: cellScale }, dt: { value: dt }
   336→        }
   337→      },
   338→      output: fbos.vel_0
   339→    });
   340→
   341→    // Color output
   342→    const outputScene = new THREE.Scene();
   343→    const outputCamera = new THREE.Camera();
   344→    const outputMesh = new THREE.Mesh(
   345→      new THREE.PlaneGeometry(2, 2),
   346→      new THREE.RawShaderMaterial({
   347→        vertexShader: face_vert, fragmentShader: color_frag,
   348→        transparent: true, depthWrite: false,
   349→        uniforms: {
   350→          velocity: { value: fbos.vel_0.texture },
   351→          boundarySpace: { value: new THREE.Vector2() },
   352→          palette: { value: paletteTex },
   353→          bgColor: { value: bgVec4 }
   354→        }
   355→      })
   356→    );
   357→    outputScene.add(outputMesh);
   358→
   359→    // Event handlers
   360→    function onMouseMove(e) {
   361→      if (!isPointInside(e.clientX, e.clientY, container)) { isHoverInside = false; return; }
   362→      isHoverInside = true;
   363→      lastUserInteraction = performance.now();
   364→      if (autoActive) { autoActive = false; isAutoActive = false; }
   365→
   366→      if (isAutoActive && !hasUserControl && !takeoverActiveVal) {
   367→        const rect = container.getBoundingClientRect();
   368→        const nx = (e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;
   369→        const ny = (e.clientY - rect.top) / rect.height;
   370→        takeoverFromVal.copy(mouseCoords);
   371→        takeoverToVal.set(nx * 2 - 1, -(ny * 2 - 1));
   372→        takeoverStartTimeVal = performance.now();
   373→        takeoverActiveVal = true;
   374→        hasUserControl = true;
   375→        isAutoActive = false;
   376→        return;
   377→      }
   378→      setMouseCoords(e.clientX, e.clientY, container);
   379→      hasUserControl = true;
   380→    }
   381→
   382→    function onMouseLeave() { isHoverInside = false; }
   383→
   384→    window.addEventListener('mousemove', onMouseMove);
   385→    container.addEventListener('mouseleave', onMouseLeave);
   386→
   387→    let running = true;
   388→
   389→    function loop() {
   390→      if (!running) return;
   391→
   392→      updateAutoDriver();
   393→      updateMouse();
   394→
   395→      // Advection
   396→      advectionPass.uniforms.dt.value = dt;
   397→      advectionPass.uniforms.isBFECC.value = BFECC;
   398→      bLine.visible = isBounce;
   399→      renderPass(advectionPass);
   400→
   401→      // External force
   402→      const fx = (mouseDiff.x / 2) * mouseForce;
   403→      const fy = (mouseDiff.y / 2) * mouseForce;
   404→      const csX = cursorSize * cellScale.x;
   405→      const csY = cursorSize * cellScale.y;
   406→      mouseMat.uniforms.force.value.set(fx, fy);
   407→      mouseMat.uniforms.center.value.set(
   408→        Math.min(Math.max(mouseCoords.x, -1 + csX + cellScale.x * 2), 1 - csX - cellScale.x * 2),
   409→        Math.min(Math.max(mouseCoords.y, -1 + csY + cellScale.y * 2), 1 - csY - cellScale.y * 2)
   410→      );
   411→      mouseMat.uniforms.scale.value.set(cursorSize, cursorSize);
   412→      Common_renderer.setRenderTarget(fbos.vel_1);
   413→      Common_renderer.render(forceScene, forceCamera);
   414→      Common_renderer.setRenderTarget(null);
   415→
   416→      // Viscous
   417→      let vel = fbos.vel_1;
   418→      if (isViscous) {
   419→        viscousPass.uniforms.v.value = viscous;
   420→        let fin, fout;
   421→        for (let i = 0; i < iterationsViscous; i++) {
   422→          fin = i % 2 === 0 ? fbos.vel_viscous0 : fbos.vel_viscous1;
   423→          fout = i % 2 === 0 ? fbos.vel_viscous1 : fbos.vel_viscous0;
   424→          viscousPass.uniforms.velocity_new.value = fin.texture;
   425→          viscousPass.output = fout;
   426→          renderPass(viscousPass);
   427→        }
   428→        vel = fout;
   429→      }
   430→
   431→      // Divergence
   432→      divPass.uniforms.velocity.value = vel.texture;
   433→      renderPass(divPass);
   434→
   435→      // Poisson
   436→      let pOut;
   437→      for (let i = 0; i < iterationsPoisson; i++) {
   438→        const pIn = i % 2 === 0 ? fbos.pressure_0 : fbos.pressure_1;
   439→        pOut = i % 2 === 0 ? fbos.pressure_1 : fbos.pressure_0;
   440→        poissonPass.uniforms.pressure.value = pIn.texture;
   441→        poissonPass.output = pOut;
   442→        renderPass(poissonPass);
   443→      }
   444→
   445→      // Pressure
   446→      pressurePass.uniforms.velocity.value = vel.texture;
   447→      pressurePass.uniforms.pressure.value = pOut.texture;
   448→      renderPass(pressurePass);
   449→
   450→      // Output
   451→      Common_renderer.setRenderTarget(null);
   452→      Common_renderer.render(outputScene, outputCamera);
   453→
   454→      rafRef.current = requestAnimationFrame(loop);
   455→    }
   456→
   457→    rafRef.current = requestAnimationFrame(loop);
   458→
   459→    // Resize
   460→    function onResize() {
   461→      if (resizeRafRef.current) cancelAnimationFrame(resizeRafRef.current);
   462→      resizeRafRef.current = requestAnimationFrame(() => {
   463→        commonResize(container);
   464→      });
   465→    }
   466→    window.addEventListener('resize', onResize);
   467→
   468→    const ro = new ResizeObserver(onResize);
   469→    ro.observe(container);
   470→
   471→    return () => {
   472→      running = false;
   473→      if (rafRef.current) cancelAnimationFrame(rafRef.current);
   474→      if (resizeRafRef.current) cancelAnimationFrame(resizeRafRef.current);
   475→      window.removeEventListener('resize', onResize);
   476→      window.removeEventListener('mousemove', onMouseMove);
   477→      container.removeEventListener('mouseleave', onMouseLeave);
   478→      ro.disconnect();
   479→      if (Common_renderer) {
   480→        const canvas = Common_renderer.domElement;
   481→        if (canvas?.parentNode) canvas.parentNode.removeChild(canvas);
   482→        Common_renderer.dispose();
   483→        Common_renderer.forceContextLoss();
   484→      }
   485→    };
   486→  }, [colors, mouseForce, cursorSize, isViscous, viscous, iterationsViscous, iterationsPoisson, dt, BFECC, resolution, isBounce, autoDemo, autoSpeed, autoIntensity, takeoverDuration, autoResumeDelay, autoRampDuration]);
   487→
   488→  return (
   489→    <div
   490→      ref={mountRef}
   491→      className={className}
   492→      style={{
   493→        position: 'relative',
   494→        overflow: 'hidden',
   495→        width: '100%',
   496→        height: '100%',
   497→        touchAction: 'none',
   498→        ...style,
   499→      }}
   500→    />
   501→  );
   502→}

   283→
   284→const LangContext = createContext()
   285→
   286→function detectLang() {
   287→  // Check URL path first: /pl → Polish
   288→  if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
   289→    const path = window.location.pathname
   290→    if (path.startsWith('/pl')) return 'pl'
   291→    if (path.startsWith('/en')) return 'en'
   292→  }
   293→  // Then check browser language
   294→  if (typeof navigator !== 'undefined') {
   295→    const browserLang = (navigator.language || navigator.userLanguage || '').toLowerCase()
   296→    if (browserLang.startsWith('pl')) return 'pl'
   297→  }
   298→  return 'en'
   299→}
   300→
   301→export function LangProvider({ children }) {
   302→  const [lang, setLang] = useState(detectLang)
   303→  const t = (key) => translations[lang]?.[key] || translations.en[key] || key
   304→  return (
   305→    <LangContext.Provider value={{ lang, setLang, t }}>
   306→      {children}
   307→    </LangContext.Provider>
   308→  )
   309→}
   310→
   311→export function useLang() {
   312→  return useContext(LangContext)
   313→}
   314→
```

     1→import { useRef, useState, useEffect } from 'react'
     2→import { motion, useInView } from 'framer-motion'
     3→import { useLang } from '../i18n'
     4→
     5→function AnimatedNumber({ value, suffix = '', prefix = '' }) {
     6→  const ref = useRef(null)
     7→  const isInView = useInView(ref, { once: true })
     8→  const [current, setCurrent] = useState(0)
     9→
    10→  useEffect(() => {
    11→    if (!isInView) return
    12→    const end = value
    13→    const duration = 2000
    14→    const startTime = performance.now()
    15→    const tick = (now) => {
    16→      const elapsed = now - startTime
    17→      const progress = Math.min(elapsed / duration, 1)
    18→      const eased = 1 - Math.pow(1 - progress, 3)
    19→      setCurrent(Math.floor(eased * end))
    20→      if (progress < 1) requestAnimationFrame(tick)
    21→    }
    22→    requestAnimationFrame(tick)
    23→  }, [isInView, value])
    24→
    25→  return (
    26→    <span ref={ref}>
    27→      {prefix}{current.toLocaleString()}{suffix}
    28→    </span>
    29→  )
    30→}
    31→
    32→export default function Stats() {
    33→  const ref = useRef(null)
    34→  const isInView = useInView(ref, { once: true, margin: '-100px' })
    35→  const { t } = useLang()
    36→
    37→  const stats = [
    38→    { value: 250, suffix: 'M+', label: t('stat_reach') },
    39→    { value: 100, suffix: 'M+', prefix: '$', label: t('stat_brand') },
    40→    { value: 1000, suffix: '+', label: t('stat_units') },
    41→    { value: 47, suffix: '', label: t('stat_countries') },
    42→  ]
    43→
    44→  return (
    45→    <section
    46→      ref={ref}
    47→      id="impact"
    48→      style={{
    49→        position: 'relative',
    50→        zIndex: 2,
    51→        borderTop: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06)',
    52→        borderBottom: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06)',
    53→      }}
    54→    >
    55→      <div style={{
    56→        maxWidth: 1400,
    57→        margin: '0 auto',
    58→        padding: '100px 48px',
    59→        display: 'grid',
    60→        gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(4, 1fr)',
    61→        gap: 48,
    62→      }}
    63→        className="stats-grid"
    64→      >
    65→        {stats.map((stat, i) => (
    66→          <motion.div
    67→            key={stat.label}
    68→            initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 30 }}
    69→            animate={isInView ? { opacity: 1, y: 0 } : {}}
    70→            transition={{ duration: 0.6, delay: i * 0.1 }}
    71→            style={{ textAlign: 'center' }}
    72→          >
    73→            <div style={{
    74→              fontSize: 'clamp(36px, 5vw, 64px)',
    75→              fontWeight: 700,
    76→              letterSpacing: '-0.03em',
    77→              color: '#fff',
    78→              lineHeight: 1,
    79→              fontVariantNumeric: 'tabular-nums',
    80→            }}>
    81→              <AnimatedNumber value={stat.value} suffix={stat.suffix} prefix={stat.prefix} />
    82→            </div>
    83→            <div style={{
    84→              fontSize: 13,
    85→              fontWeight: 500,
    86→              letterSpacing: '0.1em',
    87→              textTransform: 'uppercase',
    88→              color: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.3)',
    89→              marginTop: 12,
    90→            }}>
    91→              {stat.label}
    92→            </div>
    93→          </motion.div>
    94→        ))}
    95→      </div>
    96→
    97→      <style>{`
    98→        @media (max-width: 768px) {
    99→          .stats-grid {
   100→            grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr) !important;
   101→            gap: 32px !important;
   102→            padding: 64px 24px !important;
   103→          }
   104→        }
   105→      `}</style>
   106→    </section>

     1→import { Renderer, Program, Mesh, Triangle } from 'ogl';

     3→
     4→function hexToVec3(hex) {
     5→  const h = hex.replace('#', '');
     6→  return [parseInt(h.slice(0,2),16)/255, parseInt(h.slice(2,4),16)/255, parseInt(h.slice(4,6),16)/255];
     7→}
     8→
     9→const vert = `attribute vec2 uv;attribute vec2 position;varying vec2 vUv;void main(){vUv=uv;gl_Position=vec4(position,0,1);}`;
    10→
    11→const frag = `precision highp float;
    12→uniform float uTime;uniform vec3 uResolution;uniform float uSpeed;uniform float uScale;uniform float uBrightness;
    13→uniform vec3 uColor1;uniform vec3 uColor2;uniform float uNoiseFreq;uniform float uNoiseAmp;
    14→uniform float uBandHeight;uniform float uBandSpread;uniform float uOctaveDecay;uniform float uLayerOffset;
    15→uniform float uColorSpeed;uniform vec2 uMouse;uniform float uMouseInfluence;uniform bool uEnableMouse;
    16→#define TAU 6.28318
    17→vec3 gradientHash(vec3 p){p=vec3(dot(p,vec3(127.1,311.7,234.6)),dot(p,vec3(269.5,183.3,198.3)),dot(p,vec3(169.5,283.3,156.9)));vec3 h=fract(sin(p)*43758.5453123);float phi=acos(2.0*h.x-1.0);float theta=TAU*h.y;return vec3(cos(theta)*sin(phi),sin(theta)*cos(phi),cos(phi));}
    18→float quinticSmooth(float t){float t2=t*t;float t3=t*t2;return 6.0*t3*t2-15.0*t2*t2+10.0*t3;}
    19→vec3 cosineGradient(float t,vec3 a,vec3 b,vec3 c,vec3 d){return a+b*cos(TAU*(c*t+d));}
    20→float perlin3D(float amplitude,float frequency,float px,float py,float pz){float x=px*frequency;float y=py*frequency;float fx=floor(x);float fy=floor(y);float fz=floor(pz);float cx=ceil(x);float cy=ceil(y);float cz=ceil(pz);vec3 g000=gradientHash(vec3(fx,fy,fz));vec3 g100=gradientHash(vec3(cx,fy,fz));vec3 g010=gradientHash(vec3(fx,cy,fz));vec3 g110=gradientHash(vec3(cx,cy,fz));vec3 g001=gradientHash(vec3(fx,fy,cz));vec3 g101=gradientHash(vec3(cx,fy,cz));vec3 g011=gradientHash(vec3(fx,cy,cz));vec3 g111=gradientHash(vec3(cx,cy,cz));float d000=dot(g000,vec3(x-fx,y-fy,pz-fz));float d100=dot(g100,vec3(x-cx,y-fy,pz-fz));float d010=dot(g010,vec3(x-fx,y-cy,pz-fz));float d110=dot(g110,vec3(x-cx,y-cy,pz-fz));float d001=dot(g001,vec3(x-fx,y-fy,pz-cz));float d101=dot(g101,vec3(x-cx,y-fy,pz-cz));float d011=dot(g011,vec3(x-fx,y-cy,pz-cz));float d111=dot(g111,vec3(x-cx,y-cy,pz-cz));float sx=quinticSmooth(x-fx);float sy=quinticSmooth(y-fy);float sz=quinticSmooth(pz-fz);float lx00=mix(d000,d100,sx);float lx10=mix(d010,d110,sx);float lx01=mix(d001,d101,sx);float lx11=mix(d011,d111,sx);float ly0=mix(lx00,lx10,sy);float ly1=mix(lx01,lx11,sy);return amplitude*mix(ly0,ly1,sz);}
    21→float auroraGlow(float t,vec2 shift){vec2 uv=gl_FragCoord.xy/uResolution.y;uv+=shift;float noiseVal=0.0;float freq=uNoiseFreq;float amp=uNoiseAmp;vec2 samplePos=uv*uScale;for(float i=0.0;i<3.0;i+=1.0){noiseVal+=perlin3D(amp,freq,samplePos.x,samplePos.y,t);amp*=uOctaveDecay;freq*=2.0;}float yBand=uv.y*10.0-uBandHeight*10.0;return 0.3*max(exp(uBandSpread*(1.0-1.1*abs(noiseVal+yBand))),0.0);}
    22→void main(){vec2 uv=gl_FragCoord.xy/uResolution.xy;float t=uSpeed*0.4*uTime;vec2 shift=vec2(0.0);if(uEnableMouse){shift=(uMouse-0.5)*uMouseInfluence;}vec3 col=vec3(0.0);col+=0.99*auroraGlow(t,shift)*cosineGradient(uv.x+uTime*uSpeed*0.2*uColorSpeed,vec3(0.5),vec3(0.5),vec3(1.0),vec3(0.3,0.20,0.20))*uColor1;col+=0.99*auroraGlow(t+uLayerOffset,shift)*cosineGradient(uv.x+uTime*uSpeed*0.1*uColorSpeed,vec3(0.5),vec3(0.5),vec3(2.0,1.0,0.0),vec3(0.5,0.20,0.25))*uColor2;col*=uBrightness;float alpha=clamp(length(col),0.0,1.0);gl_FragColor=vec4(col,alpha);}`;
    23→
    24→export default function SoftAurora({
    25→  speed=0.6, scale=1.5, brightness=1, color1='#f7f7f7', color2='#e100ff',
    26→  noiseFrequency=2.5, noiseAmplitude=1, bandHeight=0.5, bandSpread=1,
    27→  octaveDecay=0.1, layerOffset=0, colorSpeed=1,
    28→  enableMouseInteraction=true, mouseInfluence=0.25
    29→}) {
    30→  const containerRef = useRef(null);
    31→  useEffect(() => {
    32→    if (!containerRef.current) return;
    33→    const container = containerRef.current;
    34→    const renderer = new Renderer({ alpha: true, premultipliedAlpha: false });
    35→    const gl = renderer.gl;
    36→    gl.clearColor(0,0,0,0);
    37→    let program, currentMouse=[0.5,0.5], targetMouse=[0.5,0.5];
    38→    const onMove = (e) => { const r=gl.canvas.getBoundingClientRect(); targetMouse=[(e.clientX-r.left)/r.width,1-(e.clientY-r.top)/r.height]; };
    39→    const onLeave = () => { targetMouse=[0.5,0.5]; };
    40→    const resize = () => { renderer.setSize(container.offsetWidth,container.offsetHeight); if(program) program.uniforms.uResolution.value=[gl.canvas.width,gl.canvas.height,gl.canvas.width/gl.canvas.height]; };
    41→    window.addEventListener('resize', resize); resize();
    42→    const geometry = new Triangle(gl);
    43→    program = new Program(gl, { vertex: vert, fragment: frag, uniforms: {
    44→      uTime:{value:0}, uResolution:{value:[gl.canvas.width,gl.canvas.height,gl.canvas.width/gl.canvas.height]},
    45→      uSpeed:{value:speed}, uScale:{value:scale}, uBrightness:{value:brightness},
    46→      uColor1:{value:hexToVec3(color1)}, uColor2:{value:hexToVec3(color2)},
    47→      uNoiseFreq:{value:noiseFrequency}, uNoiseAmp:{value:noiseAmplitude},
    48→      uBandHeight:{value:bandHeight}, uBandSpread:{value:bandSpread},
    49→      uOctaveDecay:{value:octaveDecay}, uLayerOffset:{value:layerOffset},
    50→      uColorSpeed:{value:colorSpeed}, uMouse:{value:new Float32Array([0.5,0.5])},
    51→      uMouseInfluence:{value:mouseInfluence}, uEnableMouse:{value:enableMouseInteraction}
    52→    }});
    53→    const mesh = new Mesh(gl, { geometry, program });
    54→    container.appendChild(gl.canvas);
    55→    if (enableMouseInteraction) { gl.canvas.addEventListener('mousemove',onMove); gl.canvas.addEventListener('mouseleave',onLeave); }
    56→    let raf;
    57→    const update = (time) => { raf=requestAnimationFrame(update); program.uniforms.uTime.value=time*0.001;
    58→      if(enableMouseInteraction){currentMouse[0]+=0.05*(targetMouse[0]-currentMouse[0]);currentMouse[1]+=0.05*(targetMouse[1]-currentMouse[1]);program.uniforms.uMouse.value[0]=currentMouse[0];program.uniforms.uMouse.value[1]=currentMouse[1];}
    59→      renderer.render({scene:mesh}); };
    60→    raf=requestAnimationFrame(update);
    61→    return () => { cancelAnimationFrame(raf); window.removeEventListener('resize',resize);
    62→      if(enableMouseInteraction){gl.canvas.removeEventListener('mousemove',onMove);gl.canvas.removeEventListener('mouseleave',onLeave);}
    63→      container.removeChild(gl.canvas); gl.getExtension('WEBGL_lose_context')?.loseContext(); };
    64→  }, [speed,scale,brightness,color1,color2,noiseFrequency,noiseAmplitude,bandHeight,bandSpread,octaveDecay,layerOffset,colorSpeed,enableMouseInteraction,mouseInfluence]);
    65→  return <div ref={containerRef} style={{ width:'100%', height:'100%' }} />;
    66→}
    67→
```

     1→import { useRef } from 'react'
     2→import { motion, useInView } from 'framer-motion'
     3→import { useLang } from '../i18n'
     4→
     5→export default function GrowthChart() {
     6→  const ref = useRef(null)
     7→  const isInView = useInView(ref, { once: true, margin: '-100px' })
     8→  const { t } = useLang()
     9→
    10→  // Aggressive growth curve — steep rise, no plateau
    11→  const points = [
    12→    { x: 0, y: 1 },
    13→    { x: 10, y: 2 },
    14→    { x: 20, y: 4 },   // ← "You are here"
    15→    { x: 30, y: 10 },
    16→    { x: 40, y: 22 },
    17→    { x: 50, y: 40 },
    18→    { x: 60, y: 58 },
    19→    { x: 70, y: 74 },
    20→    { x: 80, y: 86 },
    21→    { x: 90, y: 94 },
    22→    { x: 100, y: 100 },
    23→  ]
    24→
    25→  const w = 800
    26→  const h = 300
    27→  const padL = 40
    28→  const padB = 40
    29→  const padT = 20
    30→  const padR = 20
    31→  const chartW = w - padL - padR
    32→  const chartH = h - padT - padB
    33→
    34→  const toX = (v) => padL + (v / 100) * chartW
    35→  const toY = (v) => padT + chartH - (v / 100) * chartH
    36→
    37→  const pathD = points.map((p, i) => {
    38→    const x = toX(p.x)
    39→    const y = toY(p.y)
    40→    if (i === 0) return `M ${x} ${y}`
    41→    const prev = points[i - 1]
    42→    const cx = (toX(prev.x) + x) / 2
    43→    return `C ${cx} ${toY(prev.y)}, ${cx} ${y}, ${x} ${y}`
    44→  }).join(' ')
    45→
    46→  // "You are here" marker at x=20
    47→  const markerX = toX(20)
    48→  const markerY = toY(5)
    49→
    50→  return (
    51→    <section
    52→      ref={ref}
    53→      style={{
    54→        position: 'relative',
    55→        zIndex: 2,
    56→        padding: '120px 48px',
    57→        borderTop: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06)',
    58→        borderBottom: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06)',
    59→      }}
    60→    >
    61→      <div style={{ maxWidth: 1400, margin: '0 auto' }}>
    62→        <motion.div
    63→          initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
    64→          animate={isInView ? { opacity: 1, y: 0 } : {}}
    65→          transition={{ duration: 0.6 }}
    66→          style={{ textAlign: 'center', marginBottom: 60 }}
    67→        >
    68→          <div style={{
    69→            fontSize: 13, fontWeight: 600, letterSpacing: '0.15em',
    70→            textTransform: 'uppercase', color: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.3)', marginBottom: 16,
    71→          }}>
    72→            {t('chart_label')}
    73→          </div>
    74→          <h2 style={{
    75→            fontSize: 'clamp(28px, 3.5vw, 48px)', fontWeight: 700,
    76→            letterSpacing: '-0.03em', color: '#fff', lineHeight: 1.1,
    77→          }}>
    78→            {t('chart_title1')} <span style={{ color: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.25)' }}>{t('chart_title2')}</span>
    79→          </h2>
    80→          <p style={{
    81→            fontSize: 16, lineHeight: 1.7, color: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.4)',
    82→            maxWidth: 560, margin: '24px auto 0',
    83→          }}>
    84→            {t('chart_desc')}
    85→          </p>
    86→        </motion.div>
    87→
    88→        <motion.div
    89→          initial={{ opacity: 0 }}
    90→          animate={isInView ? { opacity: 1 } : {}}
    91→          transition={{ duration: 0.8, delay: 0.3 }}
    92→          style={{
    93→            display: 'flex', justifyContent: 'center',
    94→            overflow: 'hidden',
    95→          }}
    96→        >
    97→          <svg
    98→            viewBox={`0 0 ${w} ${h}`}
    99→            style={{ width: '100%', maxWidth: 800, height: 'auto' }}
   100→          >
   101→            {/* Grid lines */}
   102→            {[0, 25, 50, 75, 100].map((v) => (
   103→              <line
   104→                key={v}
   105→                x1={padL} y1={toY(v)} x2={w - padR} y2={toY(v)}
   106→                stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.04)" strokeWidth="1"
   107→              />
   108→            ))}
   109→
   110→            {/* Axis labels */}
   111→            <text x={padL - 8} y={toY(0) + 4} textAnchor="end" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.2)" fontSize="12" fontWeight="600" fontFamily="Space Grotesk">
   112→              {t('chart_now')}
   113→            </text>
   114→            <text x={w - padR + 4} y={toY(100) + 4} textAnchor="start" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.2)" fontSize="12" fontWeight="600" fontFamily="Space Grotesk">
   115→              {t('chart_future')}
   116→            </text>
   117→
   118→            {/* S-curve path */}
   119→            <motion.path
   120→              d={pathD}
   121→              fill="none"
   122→              stroke="#fff"
   123→              strokeWidth="3"
   124→              initial={{ pathLength: 0 }}
   125→              animate={isInView ? { pathLength: 1 } : {}}
   126→              transition={{ duration: 2.5, ease: 'easeInOut', delay: 0.5 }}
   127→            />
   128→
   129→            {/* Filled area under curve */}
   130→            <motion.path
   131→              d={`${pathD} L ${toX(100)} ${toY(0)} L ${toX(0)} ${toY(0)} Z`}
   132→              fill="url(#chartGrad)"
   133→              initial={{ opacity: 0 }}
   134→              animate={isInView ? { opacity: 1 } : {}}
   135→              transition={{ duration: 1.5, delay: 1.5 }}
   136→            />
   137→
   138→            <defs>
   139→              <linearGradient id="chartGrad" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
   140→                <stop offset="0%" stopColor="rgba(255,255,255,0.06)" />
   141→                <stop offset="100%" stopColor="rgba(255,255,255,0)" />
   142→              </linearGradient>
   143→            </defs>
   144→
   145→            {/* "You are here" marker */}
   146→            <motion.g
   147→              initial={{ opacity: 0, scale: 0 }}
   148→              animate={isInView ? { opacity: 1, scale: 1 } : {}}
   149→              transition={{ duration: 0.5, delay: 2 }}
   150→            >
   151→              <circle cx={markerX} cy={markerY} r="10" fill="#fff" />
   152→              <circle cx={markerX} cy={markerY} r="16" fill="none" stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.3)" strokeWidth="1.5">
   153→                <animate attributeName="r" values="14;28;14" dur="2s" repeatCount="indefinite" />
   154→                <animate attributeName="opacity" values="0.5;0;0.5" dur="2s" repeatCount="indefinite" />
   155→              </circle>
   156→
   157→              {/* Label */}
   158→              <rect x={markerX - 70} y={markerY - 50} width="140" height="28" rx="3" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.1)" />
   159→              <text x={markerX} y={markerY - 31} textAnchor="middle" fill="#fff" fontSize="14" fontWeight="700" fontFamily="Space Grotesk" letterSpacing="0.12em">
   160→                {t('chart_here')}
   161→              </text>
   162→            </motion.g>
   163→
   164→            {/* Phase labels along bottom */}
   165→            {[
   166→              { x: 15, label: t('chart_phase1') },
   167→              { x: 45, label: t('chart_phase2') },
   168→              { x: 80, label: t('chart_phase3') },
   169→            ].map((p) => (
   170→              <text
   171→                key={p.x}
   172→                x={toX(p.x)} y={h - 10}
   173→                textAnchor="middle"
   174→                fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.2)" fontSize="12" fontWeight="500" fontFamily="Space Grotesk"
   175→                letterSpacing="0.1em"
   176→              >
   177→                {p.label}
   178→              </text>
   179→            ))}
   180→          </svg>

   182→
   183→        {/* CTA */}
   184→        <motion.div
   185→          initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}

   187→          transition={{ duration: 0.6, delay: 2.5 }}

   189→        >
   190→          <a

# PESER — Powszechny Elektroniczny System Ewidencji Robotów
## Comprehensive Implementation Plan

---

## 1. Architecture Overview and Design Philosophy

PESER is a multi-page React SPA that mirrors MERA Robotics' design system exactly: React 19 + Vite + React Router 7, Tailwind CSS v4 (inline `@theme` in `index.css`, no config file), Framer Motion 12, lucide-react icons, Space Grotesk font. The aesthetic is institutional gravitas — the same pure-black/white/opacity-only palette, zero border-radius, film grain overlay, and WebGL backgrounds — but now applied to a government-grade registration system rather than a commercial product.

The site has two registers of content: a public marketing/informational layer (pages 1–3, 8–10) and a technical/standards layer (pages 4–7). This distinction drives both the routing strategy and the copy register.

---

## 2. PESER Number Format Design

The PESER number must look credibly official and carry structured machine-readable information. Proposed format:

```
PL-[YEAR][MONTH]-[CLASS]-[SEQ]-[CHK]
Example: PL-202504-H1-00001-X7
```

- `PL` — country code (ISO 3166-1, hardcoded for Poland)
- `202504` — registration year + month (YYYYMM)
- `H1` — risk class code: H1 (humanoid public), H2 (humanoid restricted), I1 (industrial cobot), I2 (industrial full), AV (autonomous vehicle), Q1 (quadruped service)
- `00001` — zero-padded 5-digit sequential registry number within that class/month
- `X7` — 2-character Luhn-style checksum computed over preceding segments

This gives 16 visible characters, groups naturally with hyphens, and is visually distinctive from PESEL (which is 11 digits, no separators). It encodes temporal, geographic, and classification data directly in the identifier itself — which makes it excellent for the "digital passport card" visualization.

---

## 3. File Structure

```
peser/
├── index.html
├── vite.config.js
├── package.json
├── .env
├── public/
│   ├── peser-logo.svg           # PESER wordmark / emblem
│   ├── robot-hero.png           # Hero robot imagery (dark-bg optimized)
│   ├── robot-id-card-bg.png     # Background texture for ID card mockup
│   └── favicon.svg
└── src/
    ├── main.jsx
    ├── App.jsx
    ├── index.css                # @import tailwindcss + @theme + film grain + scrollbar
    ├── i18n.jsx                 # LangContext/Provider/useLang — identical pattern to MERA
    ├── lib/
    │   └── supabase.js          # Nullable Supabase client (same pattern as MERA)
    ├── data/
    │   └── registry.js          # Static JSON mock of 20-30 registered robots
    ├── hooks/
    │   └── useScrollFadeIn.js   # Reusable framer-motion inView hook
    ├── components/
    │   │   # Shared primitives — copied/adapted from MERA
    │   ├── ShinyText.jsx         # Identical to MERA
    │   ├── BlurText.jsx          # Identical to MERA
    │   ├── DecryptedText.jsx     # Extracted from MERA's Hero.jsx inline version
    │   ├── AnimatedNumber.jsx    # Extracted from MERA's Stats.jsx inline version
    │   │   # Layout
    │   ├── Navbar.jsx            # PESER-branded, links to page routes
    │   ├── Footer.jsx            # PESER footer
    │   ├── PageShell.jsx         # Consistent page wrapper with padding/maxWidth
    │   ├── SectionLabel.jsx      # Reusable micro-label (UPPERCASE, 0.15em tracking)
    │   ├── HairlineDivider.jsx   # 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.06) divider
    │   │   # Backgrounds
    │   ├── LiquidEther.jsx       # Copied verbatim from MERA (Three.js fluid sim)
    │   ├── SoftAurora.jsx        # Copied verbatim from MERA (OGL GLSL aurora)
    │   ├── GridBackground.jsx    # New: CSS-only perspective grid for registry/cert pages
    │   │   # UI Atoms
    │   ├── PeserBadge.jsx        # PESER number formatted with monospace + hyphen groups
    │   ├── RiskClassBadge.jsx    # Colored status pill: AKTYWNY/ZAWIESZONY/UNIEWAŻNIONY
    │   ├── StatusIndicator.jsx   # Pulsing dot + text for robot operational status
    │   └── RobotIdCard.jsx       # The "digital passport" card visualization
    └── pages/
        ├── Landing.jsx          # Page 1: Hero + intro stats
        ├── SystemPeser.jsx      # Page 2: What/Why/How in 3 steps
        ├── Rejestr.jsx          # Page 3: Searchable registry mockup
        ├── CyfrowaTozsamosc.jsx # Page 4: DID/SSI/crypto keys
        ├── Certyfikacja.jsx     # Page 5: Zero Trust + Technical Passport
        ├── Klasyfikacja.jsx     # Page 6: Risk profiles
        ├── Transparentnosc.jsx  # Page 7: Blockchain audit log mockup
        ├── PanelRejestracji.jsx # Page 8: Registration form
        ├── DlaInwestorow.jsx    # Page 9: Investment pitch
        └── KontaktApi.jsx       # Page 10: Developer API + government contact
```

---

## 4. Routing Strategy

PESER is a true multi-page application, not a single-scroll site like MERA. React Router 7 with `createBrowserRouter` handles this. The Navbar links to path-based routes rather than hash anchors. A `PageShell` wrapper component provides consistent enter animations.

```
/             → Landing (Hero)
/system       → SystemPeser
/rejestr      → Rejestr (public database)
/rejestr/:id  → Individual robot detail page (RobotIdCard fullscreen)
/tozsamosc    → CyfrowaTozsamosc
/certyfikacja → Certyfikacja
/klasyfikacja → Klasyfikacja
/transparentnosc → Transparentnosc
/rejestracja  → PanelRejestracji
/inwestorzy   → DlaInwestorow
/kontakt      → KontaktApi
```

Language prefixing: `/pl/rejestr`, `/en/rejestr` — same URL-path detection as MERA's `detectLang()`. The `LangProvider` wraps the entire app identically.

---

## 5. Component Architecture — Detailed Specifications

### 5.1 Navbar.jsx
Identical mechanics to MERA's Navbar: fixed, transparent until scroll > 50px, then `rgba(0,0,0,0.85)` + `backdrop-filter: blur(20px)`. Navigation links are now `<Link>` components pointing to routes instead of `href="#anchor"`. The CTA button reads "ZAREJESTRUJ ROBOTA" (primary white solid) instead of "Get in Touch". The language toggle works identically.

### 5.2 LiquidEther.jsx — Hero Background
Copy verbatim from MERA. For PESER's hero, use the PESER accent colors:
```js
colors={['#1e38fa', '#5211e8', '#3a7aff']}
```
This gives the electric blue/deep violet fluid — fitting for an identity system. Same auto-demo props.

### 5.3 SoftAurora.jsx — Section Backgrounds
Copy verbatim from MERA. Use on `CyfrowaTozsamosc` and `Certyfikacja` sections:
```js
color1='#1e38fa' color2='#5211e8' brightness={0.7}
```

### 5.4 GridBackground.jsx (New Component)
A CSS-only perspective grid using SVG or CSS transform tricks — a receding grid plane that suggests a data infrastructure or blockchain ledger. This is lighter than WebGL and appropriate for the registry/transparency pages. Implemented as:
- A div with `perspective: 800px` containing an absolutely-positioned SVG grid pattern
- Grid lines at `rgba(255,255,255,0.04)` opacity
- Slow continuous `translateY` animation via CSS keyframes to create the illusion of data flowing toward the viewer

### 5.5 RobotIdCard.jsx — The Digital Passport Visual
This is the centerpiece "wow" component of PESER. It renders an ID-card-proportioned rectangle (85.6mm × 54mm ratio, approximately 560px × 352px) styled as:

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ PESER    [PL emblem]    [robot silhouette] │
│ ─────────────────────────────────────────  │
│ NUMER PESER                                │
│ PL-202504-H1-00001-X7  (monospace, large)  │
│                                            │
│ ROBOT: [Name]           KLASA RYZYKA: H1   │
│ WŁAŚCICIEL: [Corp Name] STATUS: AKTYWNY    │
│ DATA REJ.: 2025-04-15   WYGASA: 2027-04-14 │
│                                            │
│ [QR Code placeholder]  [DID hash truncated]│
└────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Styling: `background: rgba(255,255,255,0.03)`, `border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12)`, very subtle top-to-bottom gradient overlay to simulate card material. A ShinyText sweep runs across the PESER number. The card face contains a scan-line flicker animation (CSS `@keyframes`) at 5% opacity to suggest a live display terminal. On hover, the card scales to 1.02 and border brightens. A `RobotIdCard` in "compact" mode is used in the Rejestr table rows; "full" mode is used on the individual robot detail page.

### 5.6 PeserBadge.jsx
A pure formatting component. Takes a PESER number string and renders it:
- Font: monospace fallback (Space Grotesk has no mono variant; use `font-family: 'Space Mono', monospace` as secondary)
- Groups: each segment separated by a subtle `rgba(255,255,255,0.15)` hyphen
- Optionally shows a copy-to-clipboard icon (lucide `Copy` icon) on hover

### 5.7 RiskClassBadge.jsx
```
H1 → border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.3), text: white  (Humanoid Public)
H2 → border: 1px solid rgba(255,165,0,0.4),   text: rgba(255,165,0,0.8) (Humanoid Restricted)
I1 → border: 1px solid rgba(100,200,255,0.3),  text: rgba(100,200,255,0.8) (Cobot)
I2 → border: 1px solid rgba(255,80,80,0.3),    text: rgba(255,80,80,0.8) (Industrial Full)
AV → border: 1px solid rgba(180,100,255,0.3),  text: rgba(180,100,255,0.8) (Autonomous Vehicle)
```
Per the design language: no solid color fills in UI chrome. Borders and text opacity only.

### Page 1: Landing.jsx

JEDEN SYSTEM." with `ShinyText` on line 1-2, `DecryptedText` on line 3 ("BEZPIECZEŃSTWO DLA WSZYSTKICH."), then body copy, then two CTA buttons ("PRZEGLĄDAJ REJESTR" white solid + "ZAREJESTRUJ ROBOTA" ghost border)
- Right column: robot imagery with fade gradients identical to MERA's Edward image treatment

### Page 3: Rejestr.jsx
This is the most complex page. Architecture:

**Mock data source:** `src/data/registry.js` — a JS array of 25 robot objects:
```js
{
  id: 'PL-202501-H1-00001-A3',
  name: 'EDWARD WARCHOCKI',

}
```
Include a variety of classes (H1, H2, I1, I2, AV), statuses (AKTYWNY, ZAWIESZONY), manufacturers, and cities. This data is purely static JSON — no Supabase required for the public demo. Supabase integration is optional (future phase) and the `lib/supabase.js` client remains nullable.

**Layout:**
- Search input bar (full width, borderless, bottom-border-only style from MERA Contact form)
